444.reason: The line number is incremented immediately when the end of the current line is reached. However, the error should be reported on the line where the invalid character actually appears. By incrementing `curLine` here, the lexer reports the error on the next line (25) instead of the current line (24) where the single '&' is located.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This increments curLine even when curLine is already beyond the last valid line, causing getCurLine() to return a line number that is one greater than the actual error location. When an error occurs at the last character of a line, move() advances to the next line before the error is recorded, leading to an off-by-one error in line numbering.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This increments curLine immediately after finishing a line, so getCurLine() returns the next line number even when the error occurs at the last character of the current line. Thus, when an error is detected right after reading a character on line N, the processor has already advanced to line N+1, causing the reported error line to be off by one.]
